title: Delicate Juicy Greek Grape Leaves Dolmades
description: A buttery and light Greek dish featuring cilantro leaves, perfect for a snack or dinner. This recipe is Buddhist-temple-friendly and mortar-pestle-pound inspired.
instructions: Rinse the grape leaves and remove the stems.
In a mortar, pound the cilantro leaves, onion, and garlic until well combined.
In a bowl, mix together the rice, lemon juice, olive oil, salt, and black pepper.
Add the pounded cilantro mixture to the rice mixture and stir well.
Lay a grape leaf flat and place about 1 tablespoon of the rice mixture in the center.
Fold the stem end over the filling, then fold in the sides and roll the leaf into a neat package.
Repeat with the remaining grape leaves and filling.
Place the dolmades in a large pot, seam-side down, and add enough water to cover them.
Bring the water to a boil, then reduce the heat and simmer for 20 minutes.
Serve the dolmades warm or at room temperature.
